rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 17, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant The Olympic Glove & Safety Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the asbestos content issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the gloves and/or blankets at issue contained asbestos. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Olympic gloves and blankets. If the Court were to get to plaintiff's evidence, defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay would be overruled and its objections to plaintiff's Exhibit D would be sustained. As to the issue of whether plaintiff's exposure increased his risk of contracting any asbestos-related disease, defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Mr. Dotson's declaration is insufficient to demonstrate that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. (Rutherford v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 953.) However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Dr. Cohen's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was above ambient air levels, thus disputing the opinions of Mr. Dotson, and also whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
